description_long: This is a large, rectangular apartment building with a modern utilitarian design, featuring a repetitive grid of windows and balconies across its facade. The main structure consists of four prominent vertical sections, each with multiple floors, and a flat roof with several rooftop structures, likely for utilities or access. The building is primarily clad in beige and brown materials, with red and white accents on the balcony railings and window frames. A lower, attached annex extends from one side, possibly serving as a lobby, communal area, or parking garage, and features a flat roof with visible skylights or vents. The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns, and the main entrances are located at the base of the taller sections. The overall appearance suggests a mid-to-late 20th-century architectural style, commonly found in urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A large, rectangular apartment building with multiple floors, flat roofs, and a symmetrical grid of windows and balconies. The structure features beige and brown tones with red and white accents, and an attached lower annex.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with four vertical sections, rows of windows and balconies, and the attached annex on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the building and the vertical alignment of windows and balconies.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facade, similar in design to the front, with the annex now on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the annex's flat roof and the building's vertical sections.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a highly regular, vertical grid pattern, with each floor featuring evenly spaced windows and balconies aligned in columns across the facade.
